text: Agile methodology is a popular approach to project management, especially in software development. Work is divided into short cycles called sprints, usually lasting one to four weeks. Each day, the team holds a brief standup meeting to discuss progress and any obstacles. Before each sprint, the team selects tasks from a prioritized list known as the backlog. These tasks are often described as user stories, which explain a feature from the end user's perspective. The key idea is iterative development: the product is built and improved in small, repeated steps. This allows teams to adapt quickly to changes and deliver value to customers more frequently.
